title: Waiting for Results as Counting Begins May 4
summary: The vote counting process for constituencies in the Coimbatore region is scheduled to begin on May 4. Final results will be announced only after the counting is completed.

title: Coimbatore City Constituency: DMK Candidate Wins by 45,000 Votes
summary: DMK candidate defeats textile magnate-backed independent by substantial margin of 45,000 votes in closely watched urban constituency.

title: DMK Victory Celebration: Thousands Parade on Avinashi Road
summary: DMK supporters organized massive victory procession on Avinashi Road with drums, slogans, and festive atmosphere lasting till late evening.

title: Coimbatore Rural Constituency: Independent Candidate Stuns Establishment
summary: Independent farmer candidate backed by agricultural unions defeats both DMK and NDA in rural constituency with 38% vote share.

title: BJP Demands Recount in Two Constituencies Over Alleged Irregularities
summary: BJP filed formal complaint with election commission alleging counting irregularities in Coimbatore North and Coimbatore South constituencies.

title: Election Commission Accepts Recount Request; Results on May 1
summary: Election commission agreed to manual recount of votes in disputed constituencies with final results expected by May 1, 2026.

title: Textile Industry Reacts Positively to DMK Victory; Stock Rally Continues
summary: Coimbatore textile stocks surge 3.2% following DMK's pro-labor and pro-worker policies announcement. Trading volume hits 3-year high.

title: Garment Exporters Seek Meeting with Newly Elected DMK Representative
summary: Association of Garment Exporters requested urgent meeting with DMK-elected representative to discuss export promotion and duty benefits.

title: Traffic Congestion Near GMC Counting Centre: Police Deploy 100+ Personnel
summary: Heavy traffic congestion occurred near Government Medical College counting centre as supporters gathered for victory announcements. Police deployed extensive traffic management.

title: Post-Election Violence: 15 Arrested in Political Clashes in Peenya
summary: Police arrested 15 people involved in minor scuffles between competing party supporters in Peenya industrial area. No serious injuries reported.

title: Cyber Crime Unit Arrests Online Election Poll Fraudsters
summary: Cyber crime wing arrested 8 individuals running fake online exit poll websites to mislead voters during election period.

title: Heat-Related Illness Cases Rise 200% in City Hospitals
summary: Coimbatore hospitals reported 450+ heat-related illness cases on April 28, including heat stroke, dehydration, and sunburn incidents.

title: City Corporation Issues Heatwave Advisory; Warns of Peak Temperatures
summary: Coimbatore corporation issued alert predicting temperatures may touch 42°C on April 29. Advised public to avoid outdoor activities between 11 AM to 4 PM.

title: IMD Updates: Thunderstorm Possible from April 30 Evening
summary: India Meteorological Department updated forecast predicting thunderstorm and heavy rainfall from April 30 evening onwards.

title: Air Quality Index Reaches 'Moderate' Level; Concerns Over Industrial Emissions
summary: Coimbatore AQI stood at 156 (Moderate category). Environmental groups attributed rise to industrial emissions and vehicular pollution.

title: Water Supply Crisis Looms: Bhavanisagar Reservoir Level Drops Below 40%
summary: Bhavanisagar reservoir water level dropped to 38.5%, raising concerns about water availability for city's growing population during summer.

title: City Council Approves Emergency Water Management Plan for Summer
summary: Coimbatore Municipal Corporation approved emergency water management plan including rotational supply and groundwater tapping from rural zones.

title: Schools Resume After Elections; 5,000+ Students Take Board Exams
summary: Coimbatore schools reopened after election holidays with Class 10 and 12 students attending board examinations across 150 exam centers.

title: Junior Hockey Tournament Day 1: Coimbatore Academy Wins Both Matches
summary: Coimbatore Academy defeated Madurai Academy 4-2 and Salem Academy 3-1 on opening day of state-level U-19 hockey championship.

title: Badminton: Indian Women's Team Qualifies for Uber Cup Semifinals
summary: India defeated Malaysia 3-2 in Uber Cup quarterfinals. Indian team advancing to semifinals scheduled for May 2.

title: L&T Facility Opening Postponed to May 5 Due to Election-Related Delays
summary: Larsen & Toubro announced postponement of B2B electronics facility inauguration from April 28 to May 5 citing election-related administrative delays.

title: Dhanush Film 'Kara' Breaks Records: ₹5 Crore Opening in Tamil Nadu
summary: Actor Dhanush's film 'Kara' collected ₹5 crore in opening weekend across Tamil Nadu with strong performance in Coimbatore and Chennai.

title: Museum Exhibition Celebrates Coimbatore's Textile Heritage
summary: Coimbatore Art Museum opened special exhibition featuring 200 artifacts showcasing 300 years of textile manufacturing history in the region.

title: Nilgiris Tourism: Visitor Numbers Down 40% Due to Parsons Valley Fire
summary: Tourism numbers to Nilgiris from Coimbatore dropped 40% as many tourists canceled tours due to ongoing Parsons Valley forest fire.

title: NGOs Distribute Free Water and Electrolyte Drinks to Vulnerable Populations
summary: Multiple NGOs distributed free bottled water and electrolyte drinks to homeless and vulnerable population in city during extreme heatwave.

title: Residents Demand Better Cooling Shelters; Corporation Establishes 50 Relief Centers
summary: Following public demand, Coimbatore corporation established 50 air-conditioned cooling shelters across city providing free water and rest facilities.

title: Tech Park IT Companies Announce Work-From-Home Till May 15 Due to Heat
summary: Major IT companies in CODISSIA Tech Park announced work-from-home policy till May 15 to protect employees during extreme heatwave conditions.

title: Restaurants Report 60% Drop in Customer Footfall Due to Heat
summary: Restaurant industry reported significant drop in customer numbers with many establishments offering discounts on cold beverages and drinks.

title: Police Intensify Patrols After Rise in Chain-Snatching Incidents
summary: Coimbatore police increased patrol strength in city center following 12 chain-snatching cases reported in past 48 hours.

title: Heavy Traffic on Avinashi Road; Vehicle Breakdowns Increase Congestion
summary: Avinashi Road experienced severe congestion with 35+ vehicle breakdowns due to extreme heat affecting engine performance and battery life.

title: RTO Issues Advisory: Check Vehicle Conditions Before Long Drives
summary: Regional Transport Office warned drivers to inspect vehicles thoroughly before undertaking long-distance journeys during extreme heat period.

title: Power Consumption Hits Record High; Peak Load Reaches 2,400 MW
summary: State electricity board reported record peak power consumption of 2,400 MW on April 28 due to increased usage of air conditioning and cooling devices.

title: Tree Plantation Drive: 10,000 Saplings Planted Across City
summary: Environmental organization completed tree plantation of 10,000 saplings across Coimbatore in drive to combat urban heat island effect.

title: District Collector Holds Emergency Meeting on Heatwave Response
summary: District Collector convened emergency meeting with departments to review heatwave response measures and ensure citizen welfare during extreme temperatures.
